title: lathyrus cyaneus var. sessilifolius
intro: Lathyrus cyaneus var. sessilifolius is a lesser-known variety of the Lathyrus cyaneus plant, also known as the blue lathyrus or wild pea. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, with medicinal properties attributed to its seeds, leaves, and roots. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus cyaneus var. sessilifolius can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
